    CAGEMATCH Â» Events Database Â» WWE WrestleMania 36 - Tag 2

    WWE WrestleMania 36 - Tag 2

    Premium Live Event

    • Results
    • Card
    • Card with guide
    • Feuds
    • Champions
    • Additional events
    • Ratings
    • Comments
    Name of the event:
    WWE WrestleMania 36 - Tag 2
    Date:
    26.03.2020
    Promotion:
    World Wrestling Entertainment
    Type:
    Premium Live Event
    Location:
    Orlando, Florida, USA
    Arena:
    WWE Performance Center
    Attendance:
    0
    Broadcast type:
    Taped
    Broadcast date:
    05.04.2020
    TV station/network:
    WWE Network
    Commentary by:
    Byron Saxton, John Bradshaw Layfield, Michael Cole & Tom Phillips
    Theme:
    Blinding Lights (The Weeknd)
    :::: Matchguide Rating: 7.97 based on 381 votes. ::::
    WWE NXT Women's Title Match
    Charlotte Flair defeats Rhea Ripley (c) (20:27) - TITLE CHANGE !!!
    :::: Matchguide Rating: 4.77 based on 162 votes. ::::
    Singles Match
    Aleister Black defeats Bobby Lashley (w/Lana) (7:16)
    :::: Matchguide Rating: 4.86 based on 162 votes. ::::
    Singles Match
    Otis defeats Dolph Ziggler (w/Sonya Deville) (8:10)
    :::: Matchguide Rating: 4.23 based on 247 votes. ::::
    Last Man Standing Match
    Edge defeats Randy Orton (36:41)
    :::: Matchguide Rating: 4.46 based on 146 votes. ::::
    WWE RAW Tag Team Title Match
    The Street Profits (Angelo Dawkins & Montez Ford) (c) defeat Angel Garza & Austin Theory (w/Zelina Vega) (6:20)
    :::: Matchguide Rating: 5.42 based on 160 votes. ::::
    WWE SmackDown Women's Title Fatal Five Way Elimination Match
    Bayley (c) defeats Lacey Evans and Naomi and Sasha Banks and Tamina (19:20)
    - Naomi, Lacey Evans, Sasha Banks & Bayley eliminate Tamina (6:35)
    - Sasha Banks eliminates Naomi (10:20)
    - Lacey Evans eliminates Sasha Banks (13:25)
    - Bayley eliminates Lacey Evans
    :::: Matchguide Rating: 8.64 based on 592 votes. ::::
    Firefly Fun House Match
    The Fiend defeats John Cena (13:48)
    :::: Matchguide Rating: 4.53 based on 74 votes. ::::
    WWE Title Match
    Drew McIntyre defeats Brock Lesnar (w/Paul Heyman) (c) (4:35) - TITLE CHANGE !!!
    Info: This match was taped on 25.03.2020.
    Notes
    This text is hidden because it is only available in German language. Please click this line if you do not care and want to view it anyway.
    • Der Event sollte eigentlich in Tampa, Florida im Raymond James Stadium stattfinden. Aufgrund der COVID-19-Pandemie wird die Show ins WWE Performance Center verlegt und dabei erstmals vor leeren RÃ€ngen und ÃŒber zwei Tage ausgetragen.
    • Der ehemalige NFL-Spieler Rob Gronkowski fungierte als Host der Show.
    • Im Opener wurde erstmalig ein NXT Title bei WrestleMania verteidigt.
    • Mandy Rose eilte in der Schlussphase beim Match zwischen Otis und Dolph Ziggler zum Ring und schlug zunÃ€chst Sonya Deville nieder ehe sie Ziggler im Ring einen Low Blow verpasste.
    • Rob Gronkowski stÃŒrzte sich vom Balkon im WWE Perfomance Center auf einen Pulk von Wrestlern, die mit WWE 24/7 Champion Mojo Rawley brawlten, anschlieÃend konnte er Mojo pinnen, um neuer Champion zu werden.
    • Vor dem Main Event standen sich John Cena und The Fiend in einem Firefly Fun House Match gegenÃŒber. Das "Match" war im Prinzip ein groÃes Segment, in dem The Fiend Cena mit der Mandible Claw besiegen konnte, wÃ€hrend Bray Wyatt im roten Sweater den Pinfall zÃ€hlte.
    All workers
    Aleister Black, Angel Garza, Angelo Dawkins, Austin Theory, Bayley, Bobby Lashley, Brock Lesnar, Charlotte Flair, Dolph Ziggler, Drew McIntyre, Edge, John Cena, Lacey Evans, Lana, Montez Ford, Naomi, Otis, Paul Heyman, Randy Orton, Rhea Ripley, Sasha Banks, Sonya Deville, Tamina, The Fiend, The Street Profits, Zelina Vega
    6.04
    Current Total Rating (?)
    Valid votes: 286
    Number of comments: 95

    Average rating: 6.04  [286]
    Average rating in 2026: 4.82  [11]
    Average rating in 2025: 5.64  [25]
    Average rating in 2024: 6.06  [31]
    Average rating in 2023: 5.56  [27]
    Average rating in 2022: 6.24  [21]
    Average rating in 2021: 6.23  [13]
    Average rating in 2020: 6.22  [158]
    Your Options:
    • Read latest comments
    • Read all comments
    Other:
    • Details & History
    • Ratings System Rules
    • Help and formula
    Latest comments of the CAGEMATCH inmates
    SkyIcons wrote on 03.07.2026:
    [4.0] "Bray/Cena is the only thing on this show with any soul in it. Charlotte/Rhea is good too. Randy/Edge is all time bad. Everything else is just kinda there. I don't blame the lack of feeling on anyway, sign of the times and all, it does hurt this though."
    ColdSpoons wrote on 13.04.2026:
    [4.0] "Another very underwhelming pandemic era show. This had 3 matches that were a good watch. Women's five way, Rhea vs Charlotte, and the easy MOTN Firefly Fun House match. Everything else is just short and underwhelming."
    WG20 wrote on 10.04.2026:
    [4.0] "Slightly worse than night 1. The only highlights was obviously the Firefly Fun House Match and the opener of Rhea and Charlotte, the rest of the show was pretty disappointing. Especially the main event."
    Cdawg987 wrote on 11.08.2025:
    [3.0] "Wrestlemania 36 will always go down as one of the most odd Manias just due to its circumstances. The empty arena makes a lot of the show unwatchable, and you can tell it heavily affected some matches. The main event was short and to the point as I'm sure Lesnar just wanted to be out of there as quickly as possible. Still, it was a nice moment for Drew. The women's five pack challenge was boring and the lack of crowd really made those 20 minutes drag. Sadly, Otis never got the big Mania pop with his victory over Dolph and smooch with Mandy Rose. The lack of crowd also gave Orton and Edge to explore the performance center and good Lord why did they. That match was a long slog that is one of the worst Mania matches ever, at least of the big, heavily promoted matches. The few bright spots was a strong opener between Charlotte and Ripley that would've killed with a crowd. Charlotte is one of the best in the world when she's locked in, and she was fully locked in here. Bray and Cena had my MOTN with the cinematic and captivating Firefly Funhouse match, one of the most singular matches ever put on. Those two matches save this night of Mania a bit but overall, this was a weak Mania surrounded by unforseen circumstances."
    brooksavage2004 wrote on 20.07.2025:
    [3.0] "It does have the better matches compared to Day 1 with Ripley Vs Flair being fantastic and Cena Vs Fiend firefly fun house being just spectacular, but it still suffers all the same problems with a very bad undercard, especially Edge Vs Orton last man standing being all time painful bore, the severe lack of a crowd kills all the momentum and vibe that show could've had, especially for Drew's crowning moment. Overall WWE WreslteMania 36 (2020) Day 2 just like Day 1 while not an offensively bad show by anyone's fault, is still a depressing show that's way to hard to look back at given such a cursed time period."
    KKeanel wrote on 28.04.2025:
    [6.0] "Most of the matches are just fine here, with an exception of really solid opener and fantastic cinematic match. Doing a Mania in front of zero people was a risk that generally was worth of it."
    Kyrie wrote on 10.02.2025:
    "Like night one, a good and entertaining event completely kneecapped by forces out of WWE's control. But we did get the excellent Firefly Funhouse match out of it though."
    Killerman wrote on 23.12.2024:
    [3.0] "This show was way worse then night 1 in my opinion. There is only 2 good matches on night 2 instead of the 4 good matches on night 1. Those 2 good matches are Charlotte/Rhea and the Feind/Cena. One nice moment with Drew McIntyre finally winning that world title."
    UltraMark1 wrote on 24.11.2024:
    [6.0] "Not the worst mania of all time despite the lack of fans there was some good stuff in the show like the outstanding rhea charlotte match and the firefly fun house match Drew finally winning the title was a good way to end the show despite the disappointing match"
    Spireal wrote on 14.10.2024:
    [6.0] "Better in-ring than the first night . Otis/Ziggler was fun, Flair/Ripley is a very solid match (but the wrong woman won) and of course the Firefly Fun House match is a beautiful piece of storytelling. The Tag match felt really out of place. As I said for Night 1, the show remains hard to watch for today's eyes."
    Pineapple wrote on 24.07.2024:
    [5.0] "Match 1: 7/10 Match 2: 5/10 Match 3: 4/10 Match 4: 3/10 Match 5: 6/10 Match 6: 5/10 Match 7: 10/10 Match 8: 7/10 Total: 5.87/10 Overall Total: 6.00/10"
    ShaliK wrote on 05.04.2024:
    [7.0] "A night 2 on the same level as the first. A quality opener already followed by the soft underbelly of the evening. A long Edge Vs Orton which was good but also way too long. A quality Street Profits Vs Garza & Theory but a little short. Women's 5-Way is losing us. And the Wyatt Vs Cena is magnificent, followed by the fast Lesnar Vs McIntyre. The matches follow one another and the clips are not long, like the day before. It was good but the show is mainly saved by the opener and the penultimate match. MOTN: Wyatt Vs Cena (3*1/2)"
    lukerocker9 wrote on 14.09.2023:
    [6.0] "Another decent show mostly carried by 2 matches with nothing awful to drag it down too much. Ripley/Flair is by far the best match in-ring wise between both nights, with a good story told even if I would've kept the belt on Rhea (9/10) Black/Lashey is finely wrestling but heatless since it had literally no build (5/10) Otis/Ziggler had one of the more fun stories going into it and that played into making a pretty basic match enjoyable (6/10) Edge/Orton went for way too long and despite some good moments, like thesurprise RKO at the start, there was just too much filler to be good (4/10) The RAW tag title's match was again a perfectly passable match with nothing interesting to add (5/10) Women's five way also was weakened a bit by its lenght, its not bad but cutting Tamina out o fthe match and the 6 minutes she participated in this would've been better (5/10) Firefly Funhouse was more of a short film than an actual match, but a great one at that, one of the most fun and inventive things any company did during the pandemic and a great character study of Cena, and bit of Wyatt too (10/10) Lesnar/McIntyre was a strong sprint to give Drew a dominant title win, this is the match that was hurt the most by the lack of crowd out of both nights as it ould've been an awesome moment with the crowd roaring but as is its just a passable short match (5/10)"
    GMS18 wrote on 19.04.2023:
    [3.0] "A step down from the first night, this is a rough show that starts off well with the MOTN in Flair/Ripley but is mostly downhill from there. Black/Lashley and the tag titles match feel like TV matches, Otis/Ziggler is a fine enough end to a goofy feud and it's a shame there isn't a crowd to pop for the post-match stuff, Edge/Orton has a cool spot here and there but is definitely overlong and melodramatic, and the women's five-way is pretty unremarkable and dragged out to 20 minutes. I'm not even a huge fan of the Fiend/Cena business but I'm thankful for it offering something fresh. The main event is a slightly better Strowman/Goldberg from the previous night, just whatever, I guess it does the job of making Drew look good but WWE never caught the lightning in a bottle of Lesnar/Goldberg at WM33 again but not through a lack of trying. As an event on the whole, WrestleMania 36 was probably the worst since 29. The lack of crowd was of course a handicap but it was still just a poorly-booked pair of shows with only a handful of matches going for it."
    violet011 wrote on 19.04.2023:
    [3.0] "Such a dry part two to the pandemic Mania. Charlotte/Rhea and Cena/Fiend are worth watching but nothing else on this show feels important or worthy of Wrestlemania. Orton/Edge is such a chore and takes up about 40 minutes of the show, Lashley/Black had 0 build and the tag titles and SD womens title matches were just there. Otis and Mandy's moment was nice and Drew getting the win in the main was cool I guess, even if the main event was essentially a slightly better version of Braun/Goldberg. Both nights even out to probably a 4/10 for me."
    akelly99 wrote on 06.11.2022:
    [3.0] "The lack of fans will never not be jarring. Drew vs Brock was dead without a crowd, just a finisher fest in what should have been his star-making moment. Wyatt/Cena was great, but that wasn't enough to save a long long long show which didn't feel like Mania"
    arkhamoutlaw10 wrote on 01.11.2021:
    [10.0] "This is my favourite wrestling show of 2020 because it represented the scariest time in my life with such gutturally affecting isolation. Fiend vs Cena and Edge vs Orton were WWE's most radically adventurous and affecting matches in a long, long time."
    The A-Lister wrote on 05.05.2021:
    [7.0] "The better night. Not much to say, better wrestling, same division fucked, cinematic match ruled, Drew won. Mostly happy."
    Okaro143 wrote on 17.10.2020:
    [5.0] "Compared to last night, this show was nowhere as good. The only mstches worth watching are the opener and the main event. And the promo between Bray and John that was a faux match but the most entertaining thing on the show. Drew and Charlotte were great and Garza continues to impress. But overall a weak Wrestle Mania."
    AnotherSpreadsheeter wrote on 17.07.2020:
    [5.0] "A tale of highs and lows. Night two had the best match of either night, but then also two Worst Match of the Year contenders. Below average show. [Rhea Ripley vs Charlotte Flair] - 4 1/4 stars (Match of the Night). [Bobby Lashley vs Aleister Black] - 2 3/4 stars. [Dolph Ziggler vs Otis] - 1 3/4 stars. [Edge vs Randy Orton] - 3/4 of a star (Worst of the Night). [The Street Profits vs Angel Garza & Austin Theory] - 3 stars. [Bayley vs Tamina vs Naomi vs Sasha Banks vs Lacey Evans] - 2 1/2 stars. [John Cena vs Bray Wyatt] - 1 star. [Brock Lesnar vs Drew McIntyre] - 2 stars. Average match rating: 2. 25 of 5 (or 4. 5 of 10). Rounded to 5 for Cagematch."
    MaveDeltzer wrote on 01.06.2020:
    [2.0] "Three of the worst matches I've seen in years (Orton vs Edge, Cena vs The Fiend, and Lesnar vs McIntyre) on one event is not a good look, especially at Wrestlemania. Incredibly boring event capped off with a sub five-minute main event which includes 7 finishing moves and about 3 minutes of "I can't believe he kicked out" faces which make Lesnar look like a complete dummy. One of the worst events I've ever seen. I'd be surprised if we ever see a worse Wrestlemania, even considering the pandemic and lack of crowds."
    Wrestlefan20 wrote on 11.05.2020:
    [7.0] "Charlotte beating Ripley was a mistake, but this was a very good match. Definitely MOTN in terms of wrestling ability. Aleister vs Lashley was ok. It having no build-up hurt it. Otis vs Ziggler was so-so. The aftermath was a nice feel-good moment. Edge vs Orton was way too long. It had some good moments, but the length should've been cut in half. The Raw tag titles match didn't really interest me that much. The Women Smackdown's title match felt like filler. The Firefly Funhouse match, while not really a wrestling match, told a great story and had plenty of funny and serious moments. The lack of a crowd hurt McIntyre vs Lesnar. The match itself was also very short. At least McIntyre got his star-making moment."
    Damian wrote on 02.05.2020:
    [10.0] "Actually fantastic and much better than night one. My favorite of all time came back and killed it against Orton (*****), my current day favorite killed it alongside Cena (*****), and the worst match of the night was still ok (Aleister Black vs. Lashley **)."
    rykejamal wrote on 27.04.2020:
    [6.0] "Night 2 of Wrestlemania. NXT title match was a great way to open the show. Very athletic and I enjoyed the trash talking. This was very enjoyable. Not sure how I feel about the result though. Black vs Lashley was very much OK. Nothing special other than it was a solid Mania moment for Black. Next match was the same. Very much OK but the story was in the aftermath. A feel good moment. Edge vs Orton was OK at best. Way too long, got tedious and became a little boring after the halfway mark. Tag title match was another OK. Nothing special other than the aftermath. Smackdown women did good in their match. I quite enjoyed this one. Good action and story from all. Firefly Fun House was easily the best thing on the show. I loved the throwbacks, the puppets, and the work both Wyatt and Cena put into this. Amazing stuff. The build up to McIntyre main eventing Wrestlemania made him a star, and as someone who followed him from ICW, this was a huge moment for him despite the lack of an audience, and the considerable short match. It is what it is. A fairly solid show, with some drags, a couple of fun moments, a great opening match and a fantastic cinematic match."
    lee wilkinson wrote on 19.04.2020:
    [9.0] "Match wise this was one of the best manias in years. It beat the first night for me. Not a bad match on the card really. The last man standing match was great, Edge and Orton told an amazing story. The fatal five way womens match was also a highlight for me, I wasn't too invested to start with but they really won me over and the Bayley and Sasha storyline got a great new chapter. The NXT womens championship match also great, although it didn't have the outcome I wanted the match was good enough I didn't mind. Honestly the Fiend/Cena match was incredible. It was like a David Lynch film, I have no idea what this means for Cena but I loved it. All the other matches were decent enough, I'm delighted for Drew. I hope they can build him as a great champ going forward after this."
    Denpa Pope wrote on 12.04.2020:
    [5.0] "The show that is only saved by an exellent Charlotte vs Ripley and the epic and artsy fartsy masterpiece of Cena/Wyatt. Even then, with the ability to redo every move and use heavy editing, I don't feel right giving as much praise to Charlotte and Rhea as preformers either. qucik rundown: NXT Women's championship match - exellent with consistant great selling and pacing. If it were a live match, I'd rate it about * * * * 1/4* Black vs Lashley - funny ending and Lashley quips but not much else, about * 3/4* Otis vs Ziggler - didn't care for the story to begin with but such climaxes of stories of that type just don't work without a crowd going nuts in my view. Shoutout for the most passionless kiss shared between the two by the end. Edge vs Orton - about 10 minutes and 1 uncomfrotable choke spot too many. It is fine to exprience once maybe, but thinking of rewatching the match anytime ever does not give me warm fuzzy feelings. * * Women Fatal-4-Way - Did not even care enough to pay the full attention to score. Snoozefest for me Fiend vs Cena - Knacksterpiece 10/10 (not even joking) Drew vs Brock - devoid of substance wankery that only has a faint hope of working via the crowd involvement and surprise, Otherwise lowkey trash. - * * The experience was rarely flat out terrible and with 2 exellent matches, I feel comfortable rating it a 5/10"
    blackgoku wrote on 12.04.2020:
    [8.0] "The Nxt Title opener was great, Edge V Orton was absolutely amazing, Otis and Mandy had a nice moment and the match between Cena and Wyatt was outstanding. The title main event was horrible."
    20SaiDa19 wrote on 12.04.2020:
    [4.0] "Not as good as the first night, but this show still had its positives. Charlotte vs Ripley was quite easily the best actual wrestling match of the weekend and I also personally enjoyed Edge vs Orton and the Cena/Wyatt segment. The rest of the show, however, was pretty difficult to sit through, with the main event in particular being the biggest disappointment, as it was essentially the same match Goldberg and Braun had the night before."
    MrGrinningSoul wrote on 11.04.2020:
    [3.0] "Well, the smile WWE put on my face with this farce called "WrestleMania" was an incredulous one. However, it quickly froze when they decided the Chris Benoit suicide in the Last Man Standing Match, and it was fully gone by the time that silly, horribly acted skit with Cena came around. Crap Crap Crap. What a waste, what a disappointment. They should have waited doing this."
    DanTalksRasslin wrote on 10.04.2020:
    [7.0] "Night 2 was the stronger of this year's split WrestleMania, though certainly not perfect. For instance, McIntyre vs. Lesnar was, for all intents and purposes, the same match Strowman and Goldberg had the previous night, and while it was good to see Drew get his moment, it should've happened in front of a stadium crowd; ditto Otis and Mandy Rose's post-match moment. Edge vs. Orton was far too long; the end of the match told a good story but it could've been accomplished in at least half the time it took to get there. But the show certainly had strong moments as well - Charlotte vs. Rhea was probably the best traditional match of the entire weekend, Black vs. Lashley and Otis vs. Ziggler overperformed on their expectations, and the women's scramble match was pretty fun overall. And then there was the Firefly Funhouse, which was my personal favorite part of the entire weekend. To be sure, it was less of an actual match than the Boneyard, but as a deconstruction (and maybe post-mortem? ) of Cena's career, and of wrestling "heroes" and wrestling storytelling in general, it was rather fascinating and brilliantly-executed. Very cool to see WWE of all companies take a chance on something that outside the box, and on their "grandest stage" of all places (something that may not have happened had 'Mania not been forced into quarantine). Like Night 1, this was a bit of a mixed bag, but the positives of Night 2 give it a slight overall edge in my book."
    Comfortgel wrote on 08.04.2020:
    [2.0] "Starting with the first match, this event fails and continues to do so all night. Emotional moments are ruined by having no crowd, distasteful elements are performed (looking at you Edge/Orton) and the absolutely bizarre Cena segment (presented as a "match") all make for one of the worst events in WWE, if not all of wrestling history. Props to those who tried to make it work, but this event is a definite pass."
    t81487 wrote on 07.04.2020:
    [7.0] "Ripley/Flair and Wyatt/Cena were definitely the highlights of the night. Edge/Orton, while it had a good finish, was long and boring. Everything else was fine. I didn't hate the main event, but you can't help but think about how much better Drew's title win would've been in front of fans."
    Acetylsalicilique wrote on 07.04.2020:
    [7.0] "The opener was fantastic, with a disappointing winner. The Main Event was absolute garbage, with the right winner. Overall the show was nice, didn't feel like WM though. The first night was better"
    crs285 wrote on 07.04.2020:
    [7.0] "Little bit of a let down with how great night one was but once again a really good show. Ripley vs Flair: good technical match, wrong winner. Black vs Lashley: didn't have high expectations for this one but ended up liking the match, good showing for both men. Otis vs Ziggler: was worried about this one too but for me it worked and I enjoyed the post match as well. Edge vs Orton: both men gave great intensity but the match went on a little too long had some really nice spots though. Street Profits vs Garza & Theory: Good Match, the post match was more interesting, will be interesting to see what happens with Bianca Belair moving forward. Womens 5 Way: enjoyed it but can't help but think they missed an opportunity to get the Banks vs Bayley feud the right way, also didn't like that all the women's singles title went to the Horsewomen but that was more the other two than this one. Cena vs Wyatt: don't know what to say about it other than I don't understand it but I enjoyed it. Lesnar vs McIntyre: was what I expected and the right man won. Overall was happy with both nights but night 1 was better."
    storkyknight wrote on 06.04.2020:
    [3.0] "It is puzzling how so many fellow wrestling fans have some sort of strange loyalty to WWE that forces them to award high ratings. Quite simply WWE should be ashamed of this whole Wrestlemania debacle. While Night 2 was slightly better than Night 1, the whole effort seemed desperate. Charlotte and Ripley gave a really good start, with a well worked technical match. Black and Lashley, while very limited in skill, was also well worked, although Black gets a win again without doing any proper wrestling. Otis and Ziggler was surprisingly good and at this point I was beginning to feel a comeback and a good show on the cards. But Edge and Orton exposed the limitations of the last man standing stipulation and was tiresome, Orton isn't willing to take the necessary risks. The tag title match had no place on a PPV and should have been postponed. The women's match had a handful of good exchanges, but with nobody able to match Sasha's skills it was never going to be good. The final two matches were a disgrace to Wrestlemania. WWE has the resources to recover from such a shower of shit, but it is time people stopped giving them the benefit of the doubt. This weekend was an exploitative money grab and the entertainment provided falls well short of the required standard."
    kidkash wrote on 06.04.2020:
    [0.0] "That Cena vs. Wyatt crap erases everything. Everything. Immediate biggest wrestlecrap ever. Takes out all suspension of disbelief and makes the whole business look ridiculous. Just killed WWE, deservedly so. Rest of the show was just boring. Compared to Night 1, which was at least a bit watchable, this has turned out to be unwatchable. I'll never forget that Firefly thing, ever, and not in a good way, unfortunately."
    MRN wrote on 06.04.2020:
    [3.0] "Opener was the best match of the night but was still 5 minutes too long. Black vs Lashley action wasn't bad but the finish was terrible. Otis vs Ziggler wasn't good. Edge vs Orton was at least 20 minutes too long, total snooze fest. Street Profits vs Garza & Theory felt more like a kickoff match, the post match was silly as well as Garza & Theory are seemingly incapable of stopping Bianca. The women's 5 way was ok but also dragged. Fiend vs Cena was entertaining but I wouldn't even call it a match. Drew vs Lesnar was nothing more than finisher spamming."
    DrewHLMW wrote on 06.04.2020:
    [3.0] "where to start, the main event was a joke, two nights in a row with nothing but quick matches of finishers, could Brock really not give us a 10 minute match with McIntyre? its ridiculous, keep the itle far away from him for the rest of his career please. The Cena/Wyatt stuff was advertised as a wrestling match, i never saw one, i saw a nostalgic video package, it was fine but i was promised a match, where was it. Edge and Orton might be the most boring wrestlemania match of all time, it was far far too long. Black/Lashley and the raw tag titles where monday night raw matches and not worthy of wrestlemania. Charlotte and Rhea did fine but as usual rick flairs daughter crushes the up and coming talent like always its pathetic, she's just the female john cena. the smackdowns women's title was fine, nothing special and Otis finally getting the girl was cool but the match was nothing special. overall a very very poor night for the grandest stage of them all."
    Dragon Fighter wrote on 06.04.2020:
    [3.0] "This felt like a nothing show aside from the opening match, which was legitimately great. The rest was not much. The Nxt women title match was one of the best WWE women matches in a long time with tremendous storytelling, psychology, good selling as well. Black/Lashley was okay, same goes with Otis/Ziggler. Edge/Orton went too long and dragged badly without crowd reaction, no good by any means. The Raw tag and SD women match were just there, both matches happened and ended with no truly memorable moments. The Wyatt/Cena match was trash. It wasn't even a match containing some silly, boring stuff as usual from Wyatt. The WWE title match has so much potential to be a great hoss fight yet it turned out to be a better version of Golderg/Strowman. Massive disappointment."
    Angelo18 wrote on 06.04.2020:
    [2.0] "First night was so disappoiting (apart from the ladder match and personally i enjoyed also Taker's match, cause dammit he's Taker). The second night could have been brilliant, but before any further comment go and take a look to any AEW Show. From matches to characters to storytelling. Everything in AEW shines, why? Cause its not overbooked as this shame. WWE is killing WWE. Only Charlotte and Ripley has put together a wrestling match (Charlotte for me is the best female wrestler in the world). The rest was awful and repetitive. Only when something is going to come to an end and it lacks of new ideas, someone starts to overthink about his past. Goldberg and Lesnar for example: every match that they have done in the past 5 years is quite the same, you can predict easily where they're gonna go. It's impossible not to make a comparison to Jericho, Cody and Moxley, cause they are doing properly sport entertainment, not indie wrestling. The last GOOD match that WWE (Not NXT that is doing well from a matches point of view) was Bryan vs Kofi. 1 year without wrestling and also last year was a s**t. Edge vs Orton ... what a shame ... it could have been a match for the eras and not this boring mess without any sense. Wyatt vs Cena was not a match but a great promo, but dont tell me its a match. Surely the surreal atmosphere has counted in this awful event but you cannot make a great show in a so small venue, please stay in the ring and tell us what's wrestling all bout. I'll stop watching WWE, til things turn better again, cause this is only silly bulls**t. I'm sad, cause this company and this sport is one of my favourite things in life, but it doesnt make me dream anymore. Thanks AEW and in a certain way also NXT. We real old fashion fan, we must BOYCOTT WWE, for the sport we love's sake. LET US DREAM AGAIN. DAMN THIS WAS WRESTLEMANIA? PS the rest of the card is weekly show"
    ZestyZuluWarrior wrote on 06.04.2020:
    [8.0] "Definitely my favorite of the two nights. Even the worst match on the card was still passable. //// Ripley vs Flair - my MOTN easily, Charlotte did a great job working the leg and Rhea did an equally good job selling it. This was a really great physical battle that thrived with the smallest of details (Charlotte refusing to look at Rhea as she entered for example). Though I am torn on the outcome the future is still bright nonetheless. (****1/2) //// I cannot stress how little I cared about Lashley vs Black and I still to this very moment do not care but the match itself was good (***) //// Otis vs Dolph was the greatest match of all time (**********) //// Edge vs Orton was a damn war! Albeit a war that went on for far too long and dragged in parts but a war nonetheless. I hate LMS matches but really loved this one. (****3/4) //// Tag title match was a fun little sprint that was the equivalent of a RAW match. Not bad. (***1/4) //// The women's 5 way was fine, nothing exceptional but I have seen far worse. (***) //// I do not know how to rate the firefly funhouse so like the Boneyard match I will not provide a rating. However, while I hated it live I really like it now looking back. Definitely more entertaining then a singles match would have been between the two. (N/R) //// The main event was exactly what it should have been. I so wish there was a crowd because I know the pop would have been huge for Drew kicking out at 1. Good stuff. (**3/4)"
    MetalSlash wrote on 06.04.2020:
    [6.0] "Fine show for the most part, although overall Night 1 was a better show imo. The highlight of the night was easily the Firefly Fun House match between Cena & Bray Wyatt, but there were two other matches that I thought were very good: the women? s title matches. Ripley/Flair was the better of the two, but the Smackdown women put on a pretty compelling match with some good story beats and character work by Sasha & Bayley, not to mention that Lacey had one of her better performances. The low point of the show was unfortunately Edge vs Randy Orton, which was an absolute slog of a match that felt like it went on for an hour. The rest was fine, but kinda just ok. Aleister/Lashley, Otis/Ziggler, and the Raw tag title match were all serviceable, but nothing worth checking out. Drew vs Brock was another short finisher spam, which is a shame because it could have been a legit great hoss fight."
    ssamu126 wrote on 06.04.2020:
    [1.0] "Probably the worst Wrestlemania EVER. The only good things were Fiend vs Cena and Rhipley vs Charlotte, everything else was absolutely shit. Nice WM moment for Otis, Edge vs Orton was abysmal. All the rest was just nah."
    Chucky84 wrote on 06.04.2020:
    [4.0] "The first two fights and the tag team match were not too bad but Charlotte Flair is really unbearable to always win the belts, the firefly fun house is fun and very entertaining, the rest of the show is really average and the main event is pathetic so much no longer looks like wrestling but just a boring succession of finishers."
    MrOtaku2399 wrote on 06.04.2020:
    [10.0] "this was a show i had no expectations for. but in the end we got some fun matches and some very entertaining moments. i'm so happy drew won and it was great how that match turned out. the cena/bray match was the best thing i've seen from them ever. it was perfection to a T."
    SchlurboMcbundle wrote on 06.04.2020:
    [9.0] "Probably one of the better shows I? ve seen all year from any promotion. Cena-Fiend, Charlotte-Rhea, and Brock-Drew were all great in their own way, especially the first two, and the rest of the show was pretty solid, w/ Orton-Edge probably being the best match outside of those 3. Real good stuff from DA FED here, Brock Lesnars performance in that last match was really great btw, dude doesnt get near enough credit for being of the legit GOATS. Anyways, Great show!"
    gvt333 wrote on 06.04.2020:
    [3.0] "The extremely long Orton-Edge match, and the hokey Cena-Fiend skit have reduced the wrestling aspect of Wrestlemania to an all time low."
    steviecw wrote on 06.04.2020:
    [10.0] "So so so happy for Drew McIntyre. Tremendous to have a Scottish WWE Champion. The other highlights for me were the two women? s championship matches and the Firefly Funhouse match. Would like to see Lashley vs Black and McIntyre vs Brock in front of a large audience sometime soon."
    rhyno1989 wrote on 06.04.2020:
    [8.0] "Good Night 2. Not on the same level as Night 1 but still quality. Kick off show match was fine Liv/Natalya *3/4. Main Card: Rhea/Charlotte was very good, showed Rhea can hang with the elite ****1/2, Black/Lashley was solid, hopefully this is the end of the Lana partnership ***1/4, Otis/Dolph was fun, the post match angle was very well done **1/2, Edge/Orton was a total war, the brawl through the PC was very entertaining with plenty of brutal spots and the storytelling was perfect ****3/4, Street Profits/ Theory & Garza was a solid sprint with a nice post match 'debut' **1/4, Bayley/Lacey/Tamina/Naomi/Sasha was ok for what it was, decent storytelling with the finish ***, Fiend/Cena was a 10/10 total perfection segment *****, Brock/Drew capped the show off with the result that everyone wanted *. B- show"
    Valeron21 wrote on 06.04.2020:
    [5.0] "Charlotte vs. Rhea was great, the 5-way match was also fine, the other bouts were meh. Edge vs. Orton? ! Right hand punches for 35 minutes, almost no big spots, really poor. Cena vs. The Fiend? Meh! The main event was terrible."
